# A tibble: 3 × 1
  content_type
  <chr>       
1 live        
2 video       
3 short

Live Content

Live Weekday Summary

This table describes live stream performance for each publish weekday. Use n to judge how much evidence there is for each day, compare mean_views and median_views for typical performance, and use the confidence interval columns as a rough guide to uncertainty around the mean.

Live Weekdays Ranked By Median Views

This is the same weekday summary, sorted by median_views from highest to lowest. Median views are useful when a few unusually large or unusually small streams might distort the mean.

Raw Views Model Fit

This table summarizes the linear model that predicts raw view counts from publish weekday. Higher R-squared values mean weekday explains more of the variation in views, while the model p-value tests whether the weekday model improves on a no-weekday baseline.

Raw Views Coefficients

This table shows how each weekday compares with the reference weekday, Monday. The intercept is the estimated Monday view count; each weekday coefficient is the estimated raw-view difference from Monday, with the p-value indicating whether that difference is clearly distinguishable from zero in this model.

Raw Views ANOVA

This table tests the overall weekday term for the raw-views model. The weekday row asks whether publish weekday, as a group of categories, explains a meaningful amount of raw-view variation.

Log Views Model Fit

This table summarizes the model using log-transformed views, which reduces the influence of unusually large streams. Use this model when view counts are skewed and percentage-style differences are easier to interpret than raw count differences.

Log Views Coefficients

This table shows weekday differences on the log-view scale relative to Monday. Positive estimates suggest higher expected views than Monday; negative estimates suggest lower expected views, with p-values showing how strong the evidence is for each difference.

Log Views ANOVA

This table tests the overall weekday term for the log-views model. If the weekday p-value is small, weekday appears to explain differences in viewership after reducing the impact of extreme view counts.

Approximate Percent Change From Monday

This table converts the log-model coefficients into approximate percent changes from the reference weekday. Positive values indicate higher expected views than Monday, and negative values indicate lower expected views than Monday.

Raw Views By Weekday

This boxplot shows the distribution of live stream views for each publish weekday. Compare the median line and the spread within each box; wide boxes or long whiskers mean performance varies more on that day.

Mean Views With Confidence Intervals

This plot compares average live stream views by publish weekday. The vertical bars show 95% confidence intervals; overlapping intervals suggest the weekday averages may not be clearly different.

Log Views By Weekday

This boxplot shows the same weekday pattern on a log scale, which makes extreme high-view streams less dominant. Use it to compare typical performance when raw views are heavily skewed.

Video Content

Video Weekday Summary

This table describes video performance for each publish weekday. Use n to judge how many videos support each weekday estimate, compare mean_views and median_views for typical performance, and read the confidence interval columns as uncertainty around the mean.

Video Weekdays Ranked By Median Views

This is the same video weekday summary, sorted by median_views from highest to lowest. Median views are useful for ranking days when a small number of unusually strong or weak videos could pull the mean away from typical performance.

Video Raw Views Model Fit

This table summarizes the linear model that predicts raw video view counts from publish weekday. Higher R-squared values mean weekday explains more of the variation in views, while the model p-value tests whether the weekday model improves on a no-weekday baseline.

Video Raw Views Coefficients

This table shows how each weekday compares with the reference weekday, Monday. The intercept is the estimated Monday view count; each weekday coefficient is the estimated raw-view difference from Monday, with the p-value indicating whether that difference is clearly distinguishable from zero in this model.

Video Raw Views ANOVA

This table tests the overall weekday term for the raw-views model. The weekday row asks whether publish weekday, as a group of categories, explains a meaningful amount of raw-view variation.

Video Log Views Model Fit

This table summarizes the model using log-transformed views, which reduces the influence of unusually large videos. Use this model when view counts are skewed and percentage-style differences are easier to interpret than raw count differences.

Video Log Views Coefficients

This table shows weekday differences on the log-view scale relative to Monday. Positive estimates suggest higher expected views than Monday; negative estimates suggest lower expected views, with p-values showing how strong the evidence is for each difference.

Video Log Views ANOVA

This table tests the overall weekday term for the log-views model. If the weekday p-value is small, weekday appears to explain differences in viewership after reducing the impact of extreme view counts.

Video Approximate Percent Change From Monday

This table converts the log-model coefficients into approximate percent changes from the reference weekday. Positive values indicate higher expected views than Monday, and negative values indicate lower expected views than Monday.

Video Raw Views By Weekday

This boxplot shows the distribution of video views for each publish weekday. Compare the median line and the spread within each box; wide boxes or long whiskers mean performance varies more on that day.

Video Mean Views With Confidence Intervals

This plot compares average video views by publish weekday. The vertical bars show 95% confidence intervals; overlapping intervals suggest the weekday averages may not be clearly different.

Video Log Views By Weekday

This boxplot shows the same video weekday pattern on a log scale, which makes extreme high-view videos less dominant. Use it to compare typical performance when raw views are heavily skewed.

Short Content

Shorts Weekday Summary

This table describes Shorts performance for each publish weekday. Use n to judge how many Shorts support each weekday estimate, compare mean_views and median_views for typical performance, and read the confidence interval columns as uncertainty around the mean.

Shorts Weekdays Ranked By Median Views

This is the same Shorts weekday summary, sorted by median_views from highest to lowest. Median views are useful for ranking days when a small number of unusually strong or weak Shorts could pull the mean away from typical performance.

Shorts Raw Views Model Fit

This table summarizes the linear model that predicts raw Shorts view counts from publish weekday. Higher R-squared values mean weekday explains more of the variation in views, while the model p-value tests whether the weekday model improves on a no-weekday baseline.

Shorts Raw Views Coefficients

This table shows how each weekday compares with the reference weekday, Monday. The intercept is the estimated Monday view count; each weekday coefficient is the estimated raw-view difference from Monday, with the p-value indicating whether that difference is clearly distinguishable from zero in this model.

Shorts Raw Views ANOVA

This table tests the overall weekday term for the raw-views model. The weekday row asks whether publish weekday, as a group of categories, explains a meaningful amount of raw-view variation.

Shorts Log Views Model Fit

This table summarizes the model using log-transformed views, which reduces the influence of unusually large Shorts. Use this model when view counts are skewed and percentage-style differences are easier to interpret than raw count differences.

Shorts Log Views Coefficients

This table shows weekday differences on the log-view scale relative to Monday. Positive estimates suggest higher expected views than Monday; negative estimates suggest lower expected views, with p-values showing how strong the evidence is for each difference.

Shorts Log Views ANOVA

This table tests the overall weekday term for the log-views model. If the weekday p-value is small, weekday appears to explain differences in viewership after reducing the impact of extreme view counts.

Shorts Approximate Percent Change From Monday

This table converts the log-model coefficients into approximate percent changes from the reference weekday. Positive values indicate higher expected views than Monday, and negative values indicate lower expected views than Monday.

Shorts Raw Views By Weekday

This boxplot shows the distribution of Shorts views for each publish weekday. Compare the median line and the spread within each box; wide boxes or long whiskers mean performance varies more on that day.

Shorts Mean Views With Confidence Intervals

This plot compares average Shorts views by publish weekday. The vertical bars show 95% confidence intervals; overlapping intervals suggest the weekday averages may not be clearly different.

Shorts Log Views By Weekday

This boxplot shows the same Shorts weekday pattern on a log scale, which makes extreme high-view Shorts less dominant. Use it to compare typical performance when raw views are heavily skewed.
